message:
fix(sync): prevent lost wakeups and resource leaks in sync primitives

RwLock: Drop the underlying std::sync::RwLockReadGuard/WriteGuard
BEFORE notifying waiting readers/writers. Previously the notification
fired while the std guard was still held, so a woken waiter would
immediately block again on data.read()/data.write(). Wrapping the
guard in Option<> and calling .take() in Drop ensures the std lock
is released before release_reader()/release_writer() wakes waiters.

Semaphore: When an AcquireFuture is cancelled while it sits at the
front of the waiter queue, previously no one would re-wake the next
waiter, causing permits to be silently lost. Now, on cancellation,
if the removed waiter was the front entry, the next waiter (if any)
is explicitly woken so available permits are not stranded.

MPSC Reserve: Add a Drop impl for Reserve<'_, T> that removes the
waiter entry from the sender's queue when a Reserve future is dropped
before completion. Without this, a cancelled Reserve leaves a stale
entry in send_wakers, leaking memory and potentially delivering
spurious wakeups.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
